prompt:
The repository contains config.py with a `parse_config` function that turns `key=value` lines into a dict. It is too naive and crashes on real config files. Extend it so ALL of these hold: (1) blank lines and comment lines (whose first non-whitespace character is '#') are skipped; (2) surrounding whitespace is stripped from every key and value, so 'a = 1' yields key 'a' and value '1'; (3) a line that contains no '=' raises ValueError (do not crash with a ValueError from unpacking or silently ignore it — raise it deliberately); (4) only the FIRST '=' splits the line, so a value may itself contain '='; (5) a value that is entirely digits is converted to an int, otherwise it stays a string. Return the COMPLETE corrected contents of config.py using EXACTLY this format (the delimiters must be on their own lines):
<<<FILE config.py>>>
<full file content here>
<<<END>>>
Return nothing else.

inputs.name: config.py (current, naive)
